long_description:
PDNS team unable to create the VSI it is gettng failed with below error 

trans_id:b3926d1b-9c2f-4f6a-8020-66e6200e8e50
message:failed to list security groups for VSI with ID: HealthcheckVSI-eu-fr2-1:65a03f94-a3a6-4c0d-b832-72d665cd755e. RIAAS responded with 200, instead of the (expected value of) 200, however, the RIAAS response body provided no error codes
severity:error
}
Incident source: https://ibm-cloudplatform.slack.com/archives/CQWH66SBY/p1765335896123789 - Gopi Selvaraj(IaaS CORE)
short_description: Unable to create the Secrurity group
close_notes:
Issue Summary: The issue is related to the creation of Virtual Server Instances (VSIs) failing due to a problem with listing security groups. The error message indicates an issue with unmarshalling the security group collection, specifically with the protocol field, where the value "all" is not recognized.
SME Findings: The SMEs have found that the issue is caused by a change in the API version, specifically with the introduction of a new field for "all" protocol support. They have also found that the issue can be reproduced using the vpc-go-sdk.
SME Team:  NA.
Reason of Escalation: The issue was escalated because it was causing failures in the creation of VSIs, which is a critical functionality for the customers. The SMEs were unable to resolve the issue on their own and required additional assistance.
Resolution Steps: The resolution steps include: No impact for the customer need to use correct versioning VPC SG API made an version changed yesterday on SG group and We have advised customers to use API versioning always https://cloud.ibm.com/apidocs/vpc/latest#api-versioning
Signature of Issue: The signature of the issue is the error message "Invalid value for protocol. Value all is not among the allowed values".
Jira/Github/Other Tickets: The incident is related to the following tickets:

* INC10517379: VSI Creation Failure (In Progress)
* INC10517999: VSI Creation Failure - Related to INC10517379 (Resolved)

Category/Subcategory: The incident category is "Service Disruption" and the subcategory is "VSI Creation Failure".
Timeline Summary: The timeline of the incident is as follows:

* 2025-12-10 05:42:20 UTC - The issue was first reported by the customer.
* 2025-12-10 07:27:31 UTC - The SMEs started investigating the issue.
* 2025-12-10 07:42:19 UTC - The issue was escalated to the war room.
* 2025-12-10 07:49:42 UTC - The war room discussion started.
* 2025-12-10 08:05:01 UTC - The resolution steps were agreed upon.
    Checked the logs in icl
* created the and listed the SG group in via UI and API
* Paged the SDN team PDE7718241 sdn team - @Ganeshbs joined the call
* Asked Prasad from RNOS team to join the call and joined confirmed no impact for the VPC
* 2025-12-10 08:15:01 UTC - The issue was resolved No impact for the customer need to use correct versioning VPC SG API made an version changed yesterday on SG group and We have advised customers to use API versioning always https://cloud.ibm.com/apidocs/vpc/latest#api-versioning that's the code behaviour, earlier were mapping all to icmp-tcp-udp, with new API we introduced support to new protocols, so we removed all and made it icmp-tcp-udp


Resolution Runbook: The resolution runbook is not available.
Caused by Change/Incident: The issue was caused by a change in the API version.
Warroom: The war room meeting URL is https://teams.microsoft.com/l/meetup-join/19%3ameeting_NzE4NzVhZmUtMjY0Zi00N2MyLWI1ZDAtYmUxMGExNGFkYTUx%40thread.v2/0?context=%7b%22Tid%22%3a%22fcf67057-50c9-4ad4-98f3-ffca64add9e9%22%2c%22Oid%22%3a%227ebaced2-8fba-4d9b-bea9-80fd94c826de%22%7d.

The error message indicates an issue with unmarshalling the security group collection, specifically with the protocol field, where the value "icmp_tcp_udp" is not recognized.

Here's a summary of the key points:

Initial Report: The thread starts with a report of multiple PDS (Problem Description Statements) for Create VSI Failed.
Error Details: The error message is shared, indicating an issue with listing security groups for a specific VSI ID. The error mentions "unrecognized value for discriminator property 'protocol': icmp_tcp_udp".
Investigation: The team investigates the issue, checking server logs, verifying network connectivity, and running diagnostic tests.
API Version Issue: It's discovered that the issue might be related to a change in the API version, specifically with the introduction of a new field for "all" protocol support.
SDK Version: The team discusses the version of the vpc-go-sdk being used, with a suggestion to pin the version to a past date to avoid the issue.
Impact: The issue is affecting test accounts, and there's a concern about potential customer impacts. The team decides to rollout a fix, which will take around 36-48 hours.
Rollback Discussion: There's a discussion about rolling back the change, but it's decided that giving the PDNS team time to rollout the fix would be a better option.
Incident Creation: An incident (INC10517999) is created to track the issue, and a war room is set up for further discussion and resolution.
The thread involves multiple team members, including @U03RSKHFBL6, @W4HRPATTK, @W8JFAFZU2, and @WNS5GKWLX, among others, working together to resolve the issue and minimize customer impact.", "added_by": "gopi.selvaraj2@ibm.com", "added_on": "2025-12-10T07:57:41Z"}, {"comment": "PagerDuty: Q2KOCGLHTT4ODM/Global_OverlayOperations SRE_High was acknowledged/assigned to Ganesh BS.", "added_by": "snowdev@us.ibm.com", "added_on": "2025-12-10T06:56:32Z"}]
